.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-44-iterations.md:236:| Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency | `hook-state.ts` (`.tmp`), `graph-metadata-parser.ts` (pid+Date.now ms precision), command YAMLs + runtime root docs + `data-loader.ts` error message | R31-001, R31-004, R31-005, R35-003, R36-003 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/research.md:43:A copilot gpt-5.4 deep-dive on 2026-04-16 surveyed the 026 codebase and identified 19 foundational-runtime candidates (7 HIGH, 8 MEDIUM, 4 LOW) along with 4 cross-cutting systemic themes. The dive also flagged these candidates as under-scrutinized relative to their blast radius. Phase 016 exists to close that gap: run a 50-iteration deep research pass on the 4 systemic themes (silent fail-open, state contract honesty, concurrency/write coordination, stringly-typed governance) plus a transversal Domain 5 (test coverage gaps), producing actionable P0/P1/P2 findings that feed Phase 017+ remediation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/research.md:99:The research used a **5-domain investigation pattern** rather than a per-file pass. Each domain targeted a cross-cutting theme (silent fail-open, state contract honesty, concurrency, stringly-typed governance, test coverage). Iterations within a domain built on each other, with each iteration picking one novel investigation thread and reading the actual source code (not just spec docs) before emitting findings.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/research.md:188:**Thesis.** The concurrency bug class is **layered**:
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/research.md:678:### 5.5 Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/research.md:971:**File thesis.** `session-stop.ts` is the highest-leverage continuity-capture surface in the runtime. It runs at every Claude session stop; it must persist transcript fingerprints, producer metadata, spec-folder detection, and session summary to hook-state; it must trigger `runContextAutosave()` to commit the session summary to a spec-folder packet. Phase 016 found 10 distinct issues spanning fail-open patterns (D1), state contract honesty (D2), and concurrency (D3). The dominant pattern is **split-brain state** -- the stop hook makes three independent `recordStateUpdate()` calls plus a final disk reload, so the in-memory result flags can disagree with the persisted state.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/research.md:989:**File thesis.** `hook-state.ts` is the persisted temp-state substrate that binds Claude, Gemini, and OpenCode runtime hooks together. Any corruption, drift, or race here propagates through all three runtimes via session-prime, session-stop, and compact-inject. Phase 016 found 9 distinct issues, most concentrated in D2 (state contract honesty) and D3 (concurrency). The dominant pattern is **unvalidated parse + unlocked read-modify-write**.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/002-fix-memory-quality/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:55:- Proposed remediation sketch: make malformed lock metadata and unexpected non-`EEXIST` lock failures fail closed for JSON-mode runs, or surface an explicit degraded-lock state to the caller instead of silently continuing. Keep the timeout bypass behind a clearly logged, testable opt-in if cross-process concurrency really must proceed.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/002-fix-memory-quality/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:74:- Should PR-10 harden `withWorkflowRunLock()`/filesystem-lock behavior before the 9-PR content-quality train lands, or is the concurrency risk acceptable as a separate operational follow-up?
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-47-iterations.md:251:| Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency | `hook-state.ts` (`.tmp`), `graph-metadata-parser.ts` (pid+Date.now ms precision), command YAMLs + runtime root docs + `data-loader.ts` error message | R31-001, R31-004, R31-005, R35-003, R36-003 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/findings-registry.json:2234:      "downstream_impact": "A lost or interleaved `updateState()` write can cause autosave to no-op or fail while the stop hook still reports an apparently normal completion path. Callers and operators have no structured signal that the continuity save was skipped after state reread, so packet freshness can silently regress under exactly the concurrency seam this domain is auditing."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-41-iterations.md:235:| Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency | `hook-state.ts` (`.tmp`), `graph-metadata-parser.ts` (pid+Date.now ms precision), command YAMLs + runtime root docs + `data-loader.ts` error message | R31-001, R31-004, R31-005, R35-003, R36-003 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-031.md:51:- The `/tmp/save-context-data.json` issue is not just documentation drift; it is a command-surface contract reinforced by tests, which means concurrency risk is being taught to every caller.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-039.md:14:- **Downstream Impact:** A lost or interleaved `updateState()` write can cause autosave to no-op or fail while the stop hook still reports an apparently normal completion path. Callers and operators have no structured signal that the continuity save was skipped after state reread, so packet freshness can silently regress under exactly the concurrency seam this domain is auditing.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-039.md:25:- The remaining stop-hook concurrency risk is now less about byte-level races and more about **truthful result reporting**: the hook has no structured way to say whether the autosave reread actually produced a continuity write.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/FINAL-synthesis-and-review.md:586:- The foundational pass pre-announced themes that Domains 1–4 confirmed. Every subsequent domain pattern has a foundational-seam ancestor: Domain 1 silent fail-open is a deepening of R5-002 (partial persistence as success); Domain 2 state laundering is a deepening of R11-002 (legacy as success); Domain 3 concurrency is a deepening of R9-001 (collapsed vocabulary); Domain 4 governance drift is a deepening of R10-002 (unescaped prompt interpolation).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/FINAL-synthesis-and-review.md:851:| Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency | `hook-state.ts` (`.tmp`), `graph-metadata-parser.ts` (ms-precision), command YAMLs + runtime root docs + `data-loader.ts` error contract | R31-001, R31-004, R31-005, R35-003, R36-003 | D3 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/FINAL-synthesis-and-review.md:888:### 5.7 Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/FINAL-synthesis-and-review.md:1219:- **Real-load concurrency measurement.** Seven interleavings characterized; actual production frequencies not measured. Phase 017 structural fixes work regardless of frequency, but Phase 017 measurement would refine test harness priorities.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-38-iterations.md:270:A dedicated Domain 5 pass should harvest these into a concrete missing-test inventory and turn them into adversarial harnesses. The concurrency harness list now has enough specificity to stage 10+ independent tests without additional investigation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-38-iterations.md:284:| Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency                     | `hook-state.ts` (`.tmp`), `graph-metadata-parser.ts` (pid+Date.now ms precision), command YAMLs (`/tmp/save-context-data.json`), **[NEW since 32]** runtime root instructions (AGENTS/CLAUDE/CODEX/GEMINI.md), **[NEW since 32]** `data-loader.ts` error contract | R31-001, R31-004, R31-005, **[NEW since 32]** R35-003, R36-003 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-38-iterations.md:298:5. **Command-surface documentation reinforces concurrency races.** `/tmp/save-context-data.json` is documented in `memory/save.md`, `deep-research.md`, and `deep-review.md` (R32-005). **[NEW since 32]** Iteration 35 extends this to every runtime root instruction file (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CODEX.md`, `GEMINI.md`) with R35-003, and iteration 36 extends it further to the live runtime error contract in `data-loader.ts` (R36-003). The pattern now spans docs, workflow assets, runtime root instructions, and executable error messages — four independent teaching surfaces.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-38-iterations.md:579:| **[NEW since 32]** `mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts:952-993` | `insertSupersedesEdge()`'s `INSERT OR IGNORE` on `(source_id, target_id, relation)` allows multi-successor fan-out (R35-001). Does lineage analytics already break under concurrent conflict writes today, or is the hazard currently masked by low write concurrency? |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-053.md:163:1. **KQ-51-4 (if defined)**: Cross-session concurrency stress — what happens under high session churn (100+ parallel `saveState` calls within one `getStateDir()` namespace)? Does the `.tmp-<pid>-<counter>-<rand>` temp-file scheme collide? (hook-state.ts:268)
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-040.md:4:I treated Iterations 031-038 as the baseline and avoided reusing the already-claimed split-brain autosave, shared `/tmp/save-context-data.json`, and graph-metadata temp-path findings. This final pass stayed on the same concurrency domain but targeted two remaining unclaimed seams: the hook-state stale-cleanup delete path, and the governed reconsolidation scope filter that still runs outside any transaction and stitches together two unsynchronized reads.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/segment-2-synthesis.md:67:| H-56-4 Copilot silent-failure cluster | R52-P1-002 + R3-P1-001 + R1-P1-002 | **CONFIRMED** (new compound P1) | 0.83 | Copilot runtime uniquely missing three observability layers: compact-cache trustState + CP-002 closing-pass telemetry + retry-exhaustion signal. Concurrency-3 per `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override.md` concentrates per-session failures. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/segment-2-synthesis.md:115:Cluster E compound (R56-P1-NEW-002 / H-56-4): Copilot is the ONLY runtime with simultaneous gaps in (a) compact-cache trustState persistence, (b) CP-002 closing-pass telemetry (R3-P1-001), and (c) retry-exhaustion signaling (R1-P1-002). Per `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override.md`, Copilot is Phase 017's Codex-fallback runtime limited to 3 parallel dispatches — making accumulated per-session silent failures consequential per session.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/segment-2-synthesis.md:234:- Copilot-primary execution (per `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override.md` / `feedback_phase_017_autonomous.md`) MUST include T-W1-HOK-01 + T-W1-HOK-02 (Cluster E / Wave A+B) before autonomous Copilot iteration launches — else H-56-4 observability blind spot activates during Phase 017 itself.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-32-iterations.md:234:| Deterministic / shared temp path under concurrency                     | `hook-state.ts` (`.tmp`), `graph-metadata-parser.ts` (pid+Date.now ms precision), command YAMLs (`/tmp/save-context-data.json`) | R31-001, R31-004, R31-005 |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/interim-synthesis-32-iterations.md:246:5. **Command-surface documentation reinforces concurrency races.** `/tmp/save-context-data.json` is not just an accident; it is documented in `memory/save.md`, `deep-research.md`, and `deep-review.md`, with matching assertions in `generate-context-cli-authority.vitest.ts` (R32-005). The deep-dive flagged one instance; the iterations prove the pattern is workflow-wide.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/007-foundational-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-052.md:158:**Blast radius**: Copilot-driven sessions that undergo compaction lose the trust-state provenance that Claude and Gemini sessions preserve. After a Copilot compaction the recovered context has no `[PROVENANCE: ... trustState=...]` breadcrumb, so downstream `memory_context({ mode: 'resume' })` calls and session-health readouts cannot tell whether the Copilot runtime's recovered payload is `live` / `stale` / `absent` / `unavailable`. This creates a third-class experience for Copilot users even though the underlying MCP tools support the full vocabulary. If Phase 017 work adds Copilot-first autonomous iteration (per `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override.md`), this gap becomes a blocker for cross-iteration continuity.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/research/deep-research-strategy.md:3:description: Correctness, concurrency, and doc-code drift audit over the 002-continuity-memory-runtime parent packet and its four direct children (001-cache-warning-hooks, 002-fix-memory-quality, 003-continuity-refactor-gates, 004-memory-save-rewrite).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12:Forensic audit of the 002-continuity-memory-runtime packet — the parent and its four completed child phases — to surface correctness gaps, concurrency / race conditions, and doc-code drift before 026 progresses further. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:31:- `FIND-iter002-lock-after-allocation` | Severity: `P1` | File:line: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs:31-42`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs:66-100`, `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:124-170` | Description: deep-research chooses `{phaseSlug}-pt-{NN}` by counting directories before any lock is acquired, and the advisory lock file lives inside the already-chosen `{artifact_dir}`. The lock can serialize activity inside a packet, but it cannot serialize packet selection itself. | Repro / observed behavior: two initializers or a resumer plus initializer can both call `resolveArtifactRoot(...)` before `step_acquire_lock`; both decisions are made from directory count, not from an authoritative active-lineage pointer. This is the concurrency consequence of the already-logged `FIND-iter001-reducer-path-drift`, not a re-file of that bug. | Why it matters: active lineage can split or collide before advisory locking starts, so a resumer can land on a sibling packet or attach to the wrong packet generation.

The core pool shape is small: `settleItem` captures worker failures, and `runCappedPool` pumps while `active < concurrency`. I’m checking tests/callers now for any contract mismatch around ledger event durability or failure isolation.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     4	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out CLI Lineage Pool Runner                      ║
     5	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     6	// ║ Input:  CLI args (--spec-folder, --loop-type, --fanout-config-json,      ║
     7	// ║         --base-artifact-dir).                                            ║
     8	// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
     9	// ║ Exit:   0=all ok, 1=script error, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.          ║
    10	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    11	
    12	'use strict';
    13	
    14	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	// 1. IMPORTS
    16	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17	
    18	const fs = require('node:fs');
    19	const os = require('node:os');
    20	const path = require('node:path');
    21	const { spawnSync } = require('node:child_process');
    22	
    23	const {
    24	  classifyExitCode,
    25	  installSignalHandlers,
    26	  maybeThrowTestFault,
    27	  validateNamespaceValue,
    28	} = require('./lib/cli-guards.cjs');
    29	
    30	const {
    31	  runCappedPool,
    32	  appendStatusLedger,
    33	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
    34	} = require('./fanout-pool.cjs');
    35	
    36	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	// 2. TSX BOOTSTRAP
    38	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    39	
    40	const TSX_LOADER = path.resolve(
    41	  __dirname,
    42	  '..',
    43	  '..',
    44	  'system-spec-kit',
    45	  'scripts',
    46	  'node_modules',
    47	  'tsx',
    48	  'dist',
    49	  'loader.mjs',
    50	);
    51	
    52	if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED !== '1') {
    53	  const child = spawnSync(
    54	    process.execPath,
    55	    ['--import', TSX_LOADER, __filename, ...process.argv.slice(2)],
    56	    {
    57	      cwd: process.cwd(),
    58	      env: { ...process.env, DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED: '1' },
    59	      input: process.stdin.isTTY ? undefined : fs.readFileSync(0),
    60	      encoding: 'utf8',
    61	    },
    62	  );
    63	  if (child.stdout) process.stdout.write(child.stdout);
    64	  if (child.stderr) process.stderr.write(child.stderr);
    65	  process.exit(child.status === null ? 1 : child.status);
    66	}
    67	
    68	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	// 3. HELPERS
    70	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71	
    72	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    73	  const args = {};
    74	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    75	    const token = argv[i];
    76	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    77	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    78	    }
    79	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    80	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    81	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    82	      args[key] = true;
    83	    } else {
    84	      args[key] = next;
    85	      i += 1;
    86	    }
    87	  }
    88	  return args;
    89	}
    90	
    91	function inputError(message) {
    92	  const err = new Error(message);
    93	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    94	  return err;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function ensureString(args, key) {
    98	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    99	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
   100	  }
   101	  return args[key];
   102	}
   103	
   104	function jsonOut(payload) {
   105	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
   106	}
   107	
   108	// Per-kind first state-dir env var (used for lockfile isolation across same-kind replicas).
   109	const SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND = {
   110	  'cli-codex': 'SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR',
   111	  'cli-gemini': 'SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR',
   112	  'cli-claude-code': 'SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR',
   113	  'cli-opencode': 'SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR',
   114	  'cli-devin': 'SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR',
   115	};
   116	
   117	/**
   118	 * Build the "run the full loop" prompt text for a CLI lineage subprocess.
   119	 * The subprocess reads this, loads the skill, and runs the loop to convergence
   120	 * with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir overriding step_resolve_artifact_root.
   121	 */
   122	function buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage) {
   123	  const skillFile =
   124	    loopType === 'review'
   125	      ? '.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md'
   126	      : '.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md';
   127	  const agentName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review' : 'deep-research';
   128	  return [
   129	    `You are a ${agentName} agent running a fan-out lineage.`,
   130	    ``,
   131	    `Read ${skillFile} and execute the ${loopType} loop with these parameters:`,
   132	    `  spec_folder: ${specFolder}`,
   133	    `  config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: ${lineageDir}`,
   134	    `  session_id: ${sessionId}`,
   135	    `  executor: ${lineage.kind} model=${lineage.model || 'default'}`,
   136	    `  loop_type: ${loopType}`,
   137	    ``,
   138	    `The step_resolve_artifact_root step will bind artifact_dir to ${lineageDir} via the`,
   139	    `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir override — do NOT run the resolveArtifactRoot node`,
   140	    `command; bind artifact_dir directly to the override value.`,
   141	    ``,
   142	    `Run phase_init, phase_main_loop (to convergence), and phase_synthesis.`,
   143	    `Write all outputs to ${lineageDir}. Do not touch any path outside ${lineageDir}.`,
   144	    ``,
   145	    `When complete, output a single line: FANOUT_LINEAGE_COMPLETE:${lineage.label}`,
   146	  ].join('\n');
   147	}
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * Compute a generous timeout for a full lineage loop run.
   151	 * A single iteration is timeoutSeconds; a full loop is up to iterations * timeoutSeconds.
   152	 * We double it for safety and cap at 4 hours.
   153	 */
   154	function computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage) {
   155	  const iters = lineage.iterations || 12;
   156	  const perIterSecs = lineage.timeoutSeconds || 900;
   157	  return Math.min(iters * perIterSecs * 2 * 1000, 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Build the subprocess command for a CLI lineage.
   162	 * Returns { command, args, input?, env } where input is stdin content when applicable.
   163	 */
   164	function buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, resolvedSandbox, resolvedPermission) {
   165	  const kind = lineage.kind;
   166	
   167	  if (kind === 'cli-codex') {
   168	    return {
   169	      command: 'codex',
   170	      args: [
   171	        'exec',
   172	        '--model',
   173	        lineage.model || 'o4-mini',
   174	        '-c',
   175	        `model_reasoning_effort=${lineage.reasoningEffort || 'medium'}`,
   176	        '-c',
   177	        `service_tier=${lineage.serviceTier || 'default'}`,
   178	        '-c',
   179	        'approval_policy=never',
   180	        '--sandbox',
   181	        resolvedSandbox,
   182	        '-',
   183	      ],
   184	      input: prompt,
   185	    };
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  if (kind === 'cli-gemini') {
   189	    return {
   190	      command: 'gemini',
   191	      args: [prompt, '-m', lineage.model || 'gemini-2.5-pro', '-s', resolvedSandbox, '-y', '-o', 'text'],
   192	    };
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  if (kind === 'cli-claude-code') {
   196	    const args = [
   197	      '-p',
   198	      prompt,
   199	      '--model',
   200	      lineage.model || 'claude-opus-4-8',
   201	      '--permission-mode',
   202	      resolvedPermission,
   203	      '--output-format',
   204	      'text',
   205	    ];
   206	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   207	      args.push('--effort', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   208	    }
   209	    return { command: 'claude', args };
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  if (kind === 'cli-opencode') {
   213	    const args = [
   214	      'run',
   215	      '--model',
   216	      lineage.model || 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-8',
   217	      '--agent',
   218	      'general',
   219	      '--format',
   220	      'json',
   221	      '--dangerously-skip-permissions',
   222	      '--pure',
   223	    ];
   224	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   225	      args.push('--variant', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   226	    }
   227	    args.push(prompt);
   228	    return { command: 'opencode', args, input: '' };
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  if (kind === 'cli-devin') {
   232	    return {
   233	      command: 'devin',
   234	      args: [
   235	        '--print',
   236	        '--model',
   237	        lineage.model || 'swe-1.6',
   238	        '--permission-mode',
   239	        resolvedPermission,
   240	      ],
   241	      input: prompt,
   242	    };
   243	  }
   244	
   245	  throw inputError(`Unknown CLI executor kind: ${kind}`);
   246	}
   247	
   248	const {
   249	  runSalvageSweep,
   250	  extractTextFromOpencodeJson,
   251	} = require('./fanout-salvage.cjs');
   252	
   253	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	// 4. CORE LOGIC
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	
   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/feature_catalog/09--fanout/02-fanout-pool.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "Fan-out worker pool"
     3	description: "Concurrency-capped fan-out pool primitive: runCappedPool with injected worker, never-throws per-item settlement, ordered results, and JSONL status ledger."
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Fan-out worker pool
     7	
     8	---
     9	
    10	## 1. OVERVIEW
    11	
    12	Pure pool primitive — the worker function is injected by the caller so this module is
    13	agnostic to what work it is running. `runCappedPool` keeps at most `concurrency` promises
    14	in flight at any time, settles every item individually with timing metadata, emits ledger
    15	events via an optional `onEvent` callback, and returns ordered results regardless of
    16	completion order. `settleItem` wraps each worker call in a try/catch so one rejection
    17	never sinks the pool. `appendStatusLedger` and `writeOrchestrationSummary` generalize the
    18	packet-122 orchestration-status.log/orchestration-summary.json pattern into reusable helpers.
    19	
    20	### Why This Matters
    21	
    22	Reusable concurrency primitive. Any future parallel workload in the deep-loop stack can use
    23	this pool without reimplementing the concurrency cap, settlement contract, or ledger shape.
    24	
    25	---
    26	
    27	## 2. CURRENT REALITY
    28	
    29	Fully shipped in `fanout-pool.cjs`. Pure CJS (no TSX bootstrap), no CLI entry point, exports
    30	only. Concurrency is clamped to ≥1. The results array is pre-sized to preserve index order.
    31	`all_failed` is true only when every item fails — callers check this before writing a
    32	partial summary.
    33	
    34	---
    35	
    36	## 3. SOURCE FILES
    37	
    38	### Implementation
    39	
    40	| File | Role |
    41	|---|---|
    42	| `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | `runCappedPool`, `settleItem`, `buildPoolSummary`, `appendStatusLedger`, `writeOrchestrationSummary` |
    43	
    44	### Validation
    45	
    46	| File | Role |
    47	|---|---|
    48	| `tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts` | 10 tests: concurrency cap respected under gated workers, single-failure isolation, all-fail summary flag, ordered results, `onEvent` ledger emission, empty pool |
    49	
    50	---
    51	
    52	## 4. SOURCE METADATA
    53	
    54	- Group: Fan-Out
    55	- Feature ID: F024
    56	- Catalog source: `feature_catalog/09--fanout/02-fanout-pool.md`
    57	- Primary source files: `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs`

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "DLR-024 -- Fan-out worker pool concurrency cap"
     3	description: "Validate runCappedPool respects the concurrency cap, isolates per-item failures from the pool, returns ordered results, emits ledger events, and reports all_failed when every item fails."
     4	---
     5	
     6	# DLR-024 -- Fan-out worker pool concurrency cap
     7	
     8	This document captures the validation contract, execution flow, and metadata for `DLR-024`.
     9	
    10	---
    11	
    12	## 1. OVERVIEW
    13	
    14	Validates the concurrency-capped pool primitive in `fanout-pool.cjs`.
    15	
    16	### Why This Matters
    17	
    18	Reusable primitive used by `fanout-run.cjs`. If the cap is not enforced, all CLI lineages
    19	spawn simultaneously and exhaust system resources. If a single failure sinks the pool, one
    20	weak executor cancels all other lineages.
    21	
    22	---
    23	
    24	## 2. SCENARIO CONTRACT
    25	
    26	- Objective: Confirm `runCappedPool` enforces the cap, isolates failures, preserves result order, and emits JSONL ledger events.
    27	- Layer partition: pool primitive.
    28	- Real user request: `Validate the fan-out worker pool and confirm the 10 unit tests pass and match the documented concurrency and isolation contracts.`
    29	- Expected signals: Gated-worker test confirms max N in flight at any moment; failure-isolation test confirms pool continues after one rejection; `all_failed` flag is true only when every item fails; results array preserves index order.
    30	- Pass/fail: PASS if all 10 tests pass and source inspection confirms the concurrency pump logic; FAIL otherwise.
    31	
    32	---
    33	
    34	## 3. TEST EXECUTION
    35	
    36	### Prerequisites
    37	
    38	- Working directory is repository root.
    39	- `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` present.
    40	
    41	### Steps
    42	
    43	1. Inspect `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` — confirm `runCappedPool` pump loop clamps concurrency to ≥1 and pre-sizes results array.
    44	2. `bash: cd .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server && npx vitest run ../../deep-loop-runtime/tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts`
    45	3. Confirm 10 tests pass.
    46	4. Note: gated-worker test verifies `maxActive()` ≤ concurrency; failure-isolation test verifies pool continues when one item throws.
    47	
    48	### Expected Outcome
    49	
    50	10/10 pass. Pool cap respected, one rejection does not sink pool, ordered results preserved, `all_failed` correct, `onEvent` callback receives started/completed/failed events.
    51	
    52	### Failure Modes
    53	
    54	- Concurrency cap exceeded: `maxActive()` > configured cap.
    55	- One rejection propagates to pool promise: entire pool rejects instead of settling individually.
    56	- Results out of order: index not preserved across async completions.
    57	
    58	---
    59	
    60	## 4. SOURCE ANCHORS
    61	
    62	### Implementation
    63	
    64	| File | Role |
    65	|---|---|
    66	| `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | `runCappedPool`, `settleItem`, `buildPoolSummary`, `appendStatusLedger`, `writeOrchestrationSummary` |
    67	
    68	### Validation
    69	
    70	| File | Role |
    71	|---|---|
    72	| `tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts` | 10 unit tests |
    73	
    74	---
    75	
    76	## 5. SOURCE_METADATA
    77	
    78	- Group: Fan-Out
    79	- Playbook ID: DLR-024
    80	- Feature catalog entry: `feature_catalog/09--fanout/02-fanout-pool.md`
    81	- Scenario file path: `manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-pool-concurrency-cap.md`
    82	- Expected verdict mode: GREEN when 10/10 pass and source anchors agree
    83	- Wall-time estimate: 5-10 min

     1	import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
     2	import { mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs';
     3	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
     4	import { join } from 'node:path';
     5	
     6	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     7	
     8	const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
     9	const {
    10	  runCappedPool,
    11	  appendStatusLedger,
    12	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
    13	} = require('../../scripts/fanout-pool.cjs') as {
    14	  runCappedPool: (options: {
    15	    items: Array<{ label?: string } | unknown>;
    16	    concurrency: number;
    17	    worker: (item: unknown, ctx: { index: number }) => Promise<unknown>;
    18	    onEvent?: (event: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
    19	  }) => Promise<{
    20	    results: Array<{ label: string; status: string; output?: unknown; error?: { message: string } }>;
    21	    summary: { total: number; succeeded: number; failed: number; all_failed: boolean };
    22	  }>;
    23	  appendStatusLedger: (ledgerPath: string, entry: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
    24	  writeOrchestrationSummary: (summaryPath: string, summary: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
    25	};
    26	
    27	// Flush the microtask queue past any number of chained .then/.finally hops by
    28	// yielding to a macrotask — deterministic regardless of how many ticks the
    29	// pool's admission chain (.then -> .finally -> pump) takes.
    30	const flush = () => new Promise<void>((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 0));
    31	
    32	// A controllable worker: each item resolves only when its gate is released.
    33	// `entered` is append-only (worker invocation order); `inFlight` reflects the
    34	// live set (gate deleted on release) so the cap can be asserted precisely.
    35	function makeGatedWorker() {
    36	  const gates = new Map<string, () => void>();
    37	  const entered: string[] = [];
    38	  let active = 0;
    39	  let maxActive = 0;
    40	  const worker = (item: { label: string }) =>
    41	    new Promise<{ label: string }>((resolve) => {
    42	      entered.push(item.label);
    43	      active += 1;
    44	      maxActive = Math.max(maxActive, active);
    45	      gates.set(item.label, () => {
    46	        if (!gates.has(item.label)) return;
    47	        gates.delete(item.label);
    48	        active -= 1;
    49	        resolve({ label: item.label });
    50	      });
    51	    });
    52	  return {
    53	    worker,
    54	    release: (label: string) => gates.get(label)?.(),
    55	    releaseAll: () => [...gates.values()].forEach((fn) => fn()),
    56	    inFlight: () => [...gates.keys()].sort(),
    57	    entered: () => [...entered],
    58	    maxActive: () => maxActive,
    59	  };
    60	}
    61	
    62	const tmpDirs: string[] = [];
    63	function freshTmpDir(): string {
    64	  const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'fanout-pool-'));
    65	  tmpDirs.push(dir);
    66	  return dir;
    67	}
    68	
    69	afterEach(() => {
    70	  while (tmpDirs.length > 0) {
    71	    const dir = tmpDirs.pop();
    72	    if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    73	  }
    74	});
    75	
    76	describe('runCappedPool', () => {
    77	  it('never exceeds the concurrency cap', async () => {
    78	    const gated = makeGatedWorker();
    79	    const items = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e'].map((label) => ({ label }));
    80	    const run = runCappedPool({ items, concurrency: 2, worker: gated.worker });
    81	
    82	    // With cap 2, only the first two items are admitted initially.
    83	    await flush();
    84	    expect(gated.inFlight()).toEqual(['a', 'b']);
    85	    expect(gated.entered()).toEqual(['a', 'b']);
    86	    expect(gated.maxActive()).toBe(2);
    87	
    88	    // Releasing one frees exactly one slot, admitting the next item ('c').
    89	    gated.release('a');
    90	    await flush();
    91	    expect(gated.inFlight()).toEqual(['b', 'c']);
    92	    expect(gated.entered()).toEqual(['a', 'b', 'c']);
    93	
    94	    // Drain the rest, re-releasing until the pool fully settles.
    95	    for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i += 1) {
    96	      gated.releaseAll();
    97	      await flush();
    98	    }
    99	    const result = await run;
   100	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 5, succeeded: 5, failed: 0, all_failed: false });
   101	    expect(gated.entered()).toEqual(['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e']);
   102	    expect(gated.maxActive()).toBe(2);
   103	  });
   104	
   105	  it('preserves input order in results regardless of completion order', async () => {
   106	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   107	      items: [{ label: 'slow' }, { label: 'fast' }],
   108	      concurrency: 2,
   109	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => {
   110	        if (item.label === 'slow') {
   111	          await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 15));
   112	        }
   113	        return { label: item.label };
   114	      },
   115	    });
   116	    expect(result.results.map((r) => r.label)).toEqual(['slow', 'fast']);
   117	    expect(result.results.every((r) => r.status === 'fulfilled')).toBe(true);
   118	  });
   119	
   120	  it('isolates a single failing item without sinking the pool', async () => {
   121	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   122	      items: [{ label: 'ok' }, { label: 'boom' }],
   123	      concurrency: 2,
   124	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => {
   125	        if (item.label === 'boom') throw new Error('lineage executor failed');
   126	        return { ok: true };
   127	      },
   128	    });
   129	    expect(result.results.map((r) => r.status)).toEqual(['fulfilled', 'rejected']);
   130	    expect(result.results[1].error?.message).toBe('lineage executor failed');
   131	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 2, succeeded: 1, failed: 1, all_failed: false });
   132	  });
   133	
   134	  it('marks all_failed=true when every item throws', async () => {
   135	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   136	      items: [{ label: 'a' }, { label: 'b' }, { label: 'c' }],
   137	      concurrency: 2,
   138	      worker: async () => {
   139	        throw new Error('all lineages fail');
   140	      },
   141	    });
   142	    expect(result.results.every((r) => r.status === 'rejected')).toBe(true);
   143	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 3, succeeded: 0, failed: 3, all_failed: true });
   144	  });
   145	
   146	  it('resolves an empty pool with a zero summary (all_failed false)', async () => {
   147	    const result = await runCappedPool({ items: [], concurrency: 2, worker: async () => ({}) });
   148	    expect(result.results).toEqual([]);
   149	    expect(result.summary).toEqual({ total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false });
   150	  });
   151	
   152	  it('clamps a concurrency below 1 up to 1 and still completes', async () => {
   153	    const result = await runCappedPool({
   154	      items: [{ label: 'a' }, { label: 'b' }],
   155	      concurrency: 0,
   156	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => ({ label: item.label }),
   157	    });
   158	    expect(result.summary).toMatchObject({ total: 2, succeeded: 2 });
   159	  });
   160	
   161	  it('emits started + completed/failed ledger events per item', async () => {
   162	    const events: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];
   163	    await runCappedPool({
   164	      items: [{ label: 'ok' }, { label: 'boom' }],
   165	      concurrency: 2,
   166	      worker: async (item: { label: string }) => {
   167	        if (item.label === 'boom') throw new Error('x');
   168	        return {};
   169	      },
   170	      onEvent: (event) => events.push(event),
   171	    });
   172	    const byLabel = (label: string) => events.filter((e) => e.label === label).map((e) => e.event);
   173	    expect(byLabel('ok')).toEqual(['started', 'completed']);
   174	    expect(byLabel('boom')).toEqual(['started', 'failed']);
   175	  });
   176	
   177	  it('throws on invalid items or worker', () => {
   178	    expect(() => runCappedPool({ items: 'nope' as unknown as [], concurrency: 1, worker: async () => ({}) })).toThrow(
   179	      TypeError,
   180	    );
   181	    expect(() =>
   182	      runCappedPool({ items: [], concurrency: 1, worker: undefined as unknown as () => Promise<unknown> }),
   183	    ).toThrow(TypeError);
   184	  });
   185	});
   186	
   187	describe('status ledger helpers', () => {
   188	  it('appends JSONL entries to the status ledger', () => {
   189	    const dir = freshTmpDir();
   190	    const ledger = join(dir, 'nested', 'orchestration-status.log');
   191	    appendStatusLedger(ledger, { event: 'started', label: 'minimax-1' });
   192	    appendStatusLedger(ledger, { event: 'completed', label: 'minimax-1', duration_ms: 12 });
   193	    const lines = readFileSync(ledger, 'utf8').trim().split('\n');
   194	    expect(lines).toHaveLength(2);
   195	    expect(JSON.parse(lines[0])).toMatchObject({ event: 'started', label: 'minimax-1' });
   196	    expect(JSON.parse(lines[1])).toMatchObject({ event: 'completed', duration_ms: 12 });
   197	  });
   198	
   199	  it('writes the orchestration summary as pretty JSON', () => {

   202	    writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, { total: 4, succeeded: 3, failed: 1, salvaged: 1 });
   203	    const parsed = JSON.parse(readFileSync(summaryPath, 'utf8'));
   204	    expect(parsed).toEqual({ total: 4, succeeded: 3, failed: 1, salvaged: 1 });
   205	  });
   206	});

   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   261	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   262	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   263	  }
   264	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
   265	  const baseArtifactDir = ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir');
   266	
   267	  const {
   268	    parseFanoutConfig,
   269	    expandLineages,
   270	    resolveCodexSandboxMode,
   271	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
   272	    resolveGeminiSandboxMode,
   273	    resolveDevinPermissionMode,
   274	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
   275	
   276	  installSignalHandlers(() => {});
   277	  maybeThrowTestFault();
   278	
   279	  let rawConfig;
   280	  try {
   281	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
   282	  } catch {
   283	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
   284	  }
   285	
   286	  const fanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
   287	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
   288	
   289	  // Fan-out pool handles CLI lineages only; native lineages are dispatched by the YAML step.
   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };
   361	    },
   362	  });
   363	
   364	  writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
   365	    run_id: runId,
   366	    loop_type: loopType,
   367	    spec_folder: specFolder,
   368	    base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
   369	    total_cli_lineages: cliLineages.length,
   370	    ...summary,
   371	  });
   372	
   373	  const exitCode = summary.all_failed ? 3 : summary.failed > 0 ? 2 : 0;
   374	  jsonOut({ status: exitCode === 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial', run_id: runId, results, summary });
   375	  process.exit(exitCode);
   376	}
   377	
   378	main().catch((err) => {
   379	  const code = classifyExitCode(err);
   380	  jsonOut({
   381	    status: 'error',
   382	    error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
   383	    code: err && err.code ? err.code : 'SCRIPT_ERROR',
   384	  });
   385	  if (code === 1) {
   386	    process.stderr.write(
   387	      JSON.stringify({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), stack: err && err.stack }) + '\n',
   388	    );
   389	  }
   390	  process.exit(code);
   391	});

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Council Multi-Seat Dispatch                                              ║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	
     5	'use strict';
     6	
     7	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	// 1. IMPORTS
     9	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    10	
    11	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    12	// 2. CONSTANTS
    13	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    14	
    15	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16	// 3. HELPERS
    17	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    18	
    19	function isRecord(value) {
    20	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    21	}
    22	
    23	function normalizeSeat(seat, index) {
    24	  if (typeof seat === 'string') {
    25	    return { id: seat, input: { id: seat } };
    26	  }
    27	  if (!isRecord(seat)) {
    28	    throw new TypeError(`seat at index ${index} must be a string or object`);
    29	  }
    30	  if (typeof seat.id !== 'string' || seat.id.trim() === '') {
    31	    throw new TypeError(`seat at index ${index} must include a non-empty id`);
    32	  }
    33	  return { id: seat.id, input: seat };
    34	}
    35	
    36	function normalizeRoundId(roundId) {
    37	  if (typeof roundId !== 'string' || roundId.trim() === '') {
    38	    throw new TypeError('roundId must be a non-empty string');
    39	  }
    40	  return roundId;
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    44	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    45	    return value.toISOString();
    46	  }
    47	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    48	    return value;
    49	  }
    50	  return new Date().toISOString();
    51	}
    52	
    53	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    54	// 4. CORE LOGIC
    55	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56	
    57	/**
    58	 * Dispatch a single council seat and capture its result or error.
    59	 *
    60	 * Wraps the seat-specific dispatch function and records timing,
    61	 * status (fulfilled/rejected), and any error details so the
    62	 * multi-seat dispatcher can aggregate per-round results.
    63	 *
    64	 * @param {Object} params - Seat dispatch parameters.
    65	 * @param {Object} params.seat - Normalized seat descriptor with id
    66	 *   and input.
    67	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based seat index within the round.
    68	 * @param {string} params.roundId - Normalized round identifier.
    69	 * @param {Function} params.dispatchSeat - Async function that accepts
    70	 *   (seatInput, context) and returns the seat's output.
    71	 * @param {Object} params.context - Arbitrary context forwarded to
    72	 *   dispatchSeat.
    73	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO
    74	 *   timestamp for timing.
    75	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Per-seat result with seat_id, status,
    76	 *   started_at_iso, completed_at_iso, duration_ms, and
    77	 *   output or error.
    78	 */
    79	async function settleSeat({ seat, index, roundId, dispatchSeat, context, now }) {
    80	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    81	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    82	  try {
    83	    const output = await dispatchSeat(seat.input, { roundId, seatIndex: index, context });
    84	    return {
    85	      seat_id: seat.id,
    86	      status: 'fulfilled',
    87	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    88	      completed_at_iso: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
    89	      duration_ms: Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs),
    90	      output,
    91	    };
    92	  } catch (error) {
    93	    return {
    94	      seat_id: seat.id,
    95	      status: 'rejected',
    96	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    97	      completed_at_iso: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
    98	      duration_ms: Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs),
    99	      error: {
   100	        name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   101	        message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   102	      },
   103	    };
   104	  }
   105	}
   106	
   107	/**
   108	 * Dispatch all council seats for a single deliberation round in parallel.
   109	 *
   110	 * Normalizes inputs, dispatches each seat concurrently via
   111	 * Promise.all, and returns an aggregated round result with per-seat
   112	 * outcomes and a summary (total / succeeded / failed / all_failed).
   113	 *
   114	 * @param {Object} options - Dispatch configuration.
   115	 * @param {string} options.roundId - Round identifier (non-empty string).
   116	 * @param {Array<string|Object>} options.seats - Array of seat
   117	 *   descriptors (string IDs or objects with id and input).
   118	 * @param {Function} options.dispatchSeat - Async dispatch function
   119	 *   invoked per seat.
   120	 * @param {Object} [options.context={}] - Arbitrary context forwarded
   121	 *   to each seat dispatch.
   122	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date or ISO
   123	 *   timestamp (default: () => new Date()).
   124	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Round result with round_id,
   125	 *   started_at_iso, completed_at_iso, results array, and summary.
   126	 * @throws {TypeError} If options is not an object, roundId is invalid,
   127	 *   seats is empty, or dispatchSeat is not a function.
   128	 */
   129	async function dispatchCouncilSeats(options) {
   130	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   131	    throw new TypeError('dispatchCouncilSeats options must be an object');
   132	  }
   133	  const roundId = normalizeRoundId(options.roundId);
   134	  if (!Array.isArray(options.seats) || options.seats.length === 0) {
   135	    throw new TypeError('seats must be a non-empty array');
   136	  }
   137	  if (typeof options.dispatchSeat !== 'function') {
   138	    throw new TypeError('dispatchSeat must be a function');
   139	  }
   140	
   141	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   142	  const seats = options.seats.map(normalizeSeat);
   143	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   144	  const results = await Promise.all(
   145	    seats.map((seat, index) => settleSeat({
   146	      seat,
   147	      index,
   148	      roundId,
   149	      dispatchSeat: options.dispatchSeat,
   150	      context: options.context || {},
   151	      now,
   152	    })),
   153	  );
   154	  const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   155	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   156	  const failed = results.length - succeeded;
   157	
   158	  return {
   159	    round_id: roundId,
   160	    started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   161	    completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   162	    results,
   163	    summary: {
   164	      total: results.length,
   165	      succeeded,
   166	      failed,
   167	      all_failed: failed === results.length,
   168	    },
   169	  };
   170	}
   171	
   172	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   173	// 5. EXPORTS
   174	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   175	
   176	module.exports = {
   177	  dispatchCouncilSeats,
   178	};

    21	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    22	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->
    23	
    24	# Phase 002 — Capped worker pool + status ledger
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Provide the concurrency-capped fan-out orchestrator primitive (the one thing the council dispatcher lacks).
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- New `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` (script-entry contract like `scripts/convergence.cjs`: tsx bootstrap, JSON-out, exit 0/1/2/3 via `scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs`).
    31	- `runCappedPool({items, concurrency, worker})`: ≤concurrency promises in flight; per-item try/catch (one failure never sinks the pool); allSettled-style results with timing/status (mirror `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs`).
    32	- Status ledger: `{artifact_dir}/orchestration-status.log` (JSONL per-lineage events) + `{artifact_dir}/orchestration-summary.json` (`total/succeeded/failed/salvaged/perLineage[]`).
    33	
    34	## Success
    35	- New `tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts` (modeled on `tests/council/multi-seat-dispatch.vitest.ts`): cap respected; isolation on reject; all-fail summary; ledger shape. Full vitest green.
    36	
    37	## Out of scope
    38	Per-lineage spawn (003); the `worker` is mockable here.

codex
## Focus
fanout-pool.cjs correctness
## Findings
P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs:185, The defensive rejection handler calls `labelFor(items[index], index)` again; if the original settlement failure was caused by a throwing label accessor, this catch throws too, leaving the ignored `.finally()` chain to emit an unhandled rejection while the pool still resolves with an unset result. Fix: compute a safe fallback label without re-reading the item, or wrap fallback result construction in its own try/catch and terminate the chain.
## Verdict
Concurrency-cap enforcement and normal worker rejection isolation look sound; one defensive error path still violates the no-unhandled-rejection contract.
Review verdict: CONDITIONAL
